summary: Docker-based PoC demonstrates ECDSA signature malleability: both (r, s) and (r, n-s) signatures pass verification for the same message, violating low-s requirement.
expected_security_impact: Two distinct signatures should pass verification for the same message, demonstrating signature malleability and enabling blockchain transaction manipulation.
actual_observed_result: Exploit script generated ECDSA signature (r, s), computed malleable variant (r, n-s), and both signatures passed verification for the same message. Output confirms low-s requirement violation.
verification_method: Automated verification: generate signature (r, s), compute (r, n-s), verify both pass for same message, check that one violates low-s (s > n/2).
evidence_produced_by_run: Console output showing both signature verifications passed
Low-s violation detected: one signature has s > n/2

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones jsrsasign repository on host, checks out vulnerable version, and mounts read-only at /target-repo.
